docs.title: django-components-lite
docs.text:
Lightweight reusable template components for Django.

An exploratory fork of django-components that strips the library down to its core: define a component with a Python class and a template, use it in your templates, and render.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Component classes with Python logic and Django templates
  • {% component %} / {% endcomponent %} template tags
  • Slots and fills ({% slot %}, {% fill %})
  • Component autodiscovery
  • Component registry
  • Static file handling (JS/CSS)
  • Isolated component context
  • HTML attribute rendering utilities

docs.title: Attribution
docs.text:
This project is built on the work of the django-components project by Emil Stenstrom, Juro Oravec, and all contributors.

If you want a mature, full-featured component library for Django, use django-components.

docs.title: Install the package
docs.text:
pip install django-components-lite

Or with uv:

uv add django-components-lite

docs.title: Add to Django settings
docs.text:
INSTALLED_APPS = [
    # ...
    "django_components_lite",
]

docs.title: Configure templates
docs.text:
Add the component directories to your template loaders:

TEMPLATES = [
    {
        "BACKEND": "django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates",
        "DIRS": [],
        "OPTIONS": {
            "context_processors": [
                "django.template.context_processors.debug",
                "django.template.context_processors.request",
                "django.contrib.auth.context_processors.auth",
                "django.contrib.messages.context_processors.messages",
            ],
            "loaders": [
                (
                    "django.template.loaders.cached.Loader",
                    [
                        "django.template.loaders.filesystem.Loader",
                        "django.template.loaders.app_directories.Loader",
                        "django_components_lite.template_loader.Loader",
                    ],
                ),
            ],
        },
    },
]

docs.title: Configure component directories
docs.text:
By default, components are discovered in components/ directories within each app and in a root-level components/ directory.

You can customize this via settings:

from django_components_lite import ComponentsSettings

COMPONENTS = ComponentsSettings(
    dirs=[BASE_DIR / "components"],
    app_dirs=["components"],
)

docs.title: Create a component
docs.text:
Create a file at components/greeting/greeting.py:

from django_components_lite import Component, register

@register("greeting")
class Greeting(Component):
    template_name = "greeting/greeting.html"

    def get_context_data(self, name="World"):
        return {"name": name}

Create the template at components/greeting/greeting.html:

<div class="greeting">
  Hello, {{ name }}!
  {% load component_tags %}
  {% slot "extra" %}{% endslot %}
</div>

docs.title: Use it in a template
docs.text:
{% load component_tags %}

{% component "greeting" name="Django" %}
  {% fill "extra" %}
    <p>Welcome to components!</p>
  {% endfill %}
{% endcomponent %}

This renders:

<div class="greeting">
  Hello, Django!
  <p>Welcome to components!</p>
</div>

docs.title: Adding CSS and JS
docs.text:
Place static files next to your component:

components/greeting/
    greeting.py
    greeting.html
    greeting.css
    greeting.js

Define them in your component class:

@register("greeting")
class Greeting(Component):
    template_name = "greeting/greeting.html"

    class Media:
        css = "greeting/greeting.css"
        js = "greeting/greeting.js"

    def get_context_data(self, name="World"):
        return {"name": name}

The CSS and JS tags are automatically prepended to the component's rendered HTML.

docs.title: Component
docs.text:
The base class for all components. Subclass this to create your own components.

from django_components_lite import Component, register

@register("my_component")
class MyComponent(Component):
    template_name = "my_component/my_component.html"

    def get_context_data(self, **kwargs):
        return {"key": "value"}

Key attributes:

  • template_name - Path to the component's template file
  • template - Inline template string (alternative to template_name)

Key methods:

  • get_context_data(**kwargs) - Return a dict of context variables for the template
  • render(kwargs=None, slots=None) - Render the component to an HTML string (class method)
  • render_to_response(kwargs=None, slots=None) - Render and return an HttpResponse (class method)

docs.title: Registration
docs.text:
from django_components_lite import register

@register("name")
class MyComponent(Component):
    ...

Or register manually:

from django_components_lite import registry

registry.register("name", MyComponent)
registry.unregister("name")
registry.get("name")  # Returns the component class
registry.all()  # Returns dict of all registered components

docs.title: Settings
docs.text:
from django_components_lite import ComponentsSettings

COMPONENTS = ComponentsSettings(
    autodiscover=True,
    dirs=[BASE_DIR / "components"],
    app_dirs=["components"],
)

See Settings for all options.

docs.title: Template Tags
docs.text:
Available after {% load component_tags %}:

Tag Description {% component "name" %}...{% endcomponent %} Render a component {% slot "name" %}...{% endslot %} Define a slot in a component template {% fill "name" %}...{% endfill %} Fill a slot when using a component

docs.title: 0.1.0a1
docs.text: Initial release. Forked from django-components v0.143.0.

docs.title: Removed from upstream
docs.text:
• Extension system
  • Built-in components (DynamicComponent, ErrorFallback)
  • Component caching
  • Provide/Inject system (inject() method, {% provide %} tag)
  • Template expressions
  • Management commands
  • JS/CSS data methods and dependency management
  • Type validation (Args/Kwargs/Slots/TemplateData)
  • on_render() generator system and deferred rendering
  • context_behavior setting (always isolated)
  • Tag formatters
  • Component views and URLs
  • libraries setting and import_libraries() function
  • reload_on_file_change setting
  • All deprecated setting aliases (debug_highlight_components, debug_highlight_slots, dynamic_component_name, template_cache_size, reload_on_template_change, forbidden_static_files)

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Renamed package to django-components-lite (import: django_components_lite)
  • Python 3.12+ and Django 5.2+ only
  • Switched build system from setuptools to hatchling
  • Switched from pip/tox to uv
  • Moved source from src/ to flat layout
  • Moved sampleproject/ to examples/simple/
  • Rewrote documentation from scratch
  • Simplified CI workflows (matching django-cachex patterns)
  • Automatic tagging and PyPI publishing on version bump

docs.title: Defining a component
docs.text:
A component is a Python class that extends Component:

from django_components_lite import Component, register

@register("card")
class Card(Component):
    template_name = "card/card.html"

    def get_context_data(self, title, body=""):
        return {"title": title, "body": body}

docs.title: Template
docs.text:
The template uses standard Django template syntax plus component-specific tags:

{% load component_tags %}
<div class="card">
  <h2>{{ title }}</h2>
  <div class="card-body">
    {% slot "content" %}{{ body }}{% endslot %}
  </div>
</div>

docs.title: Rendering
docs.text:
From a template:

{% load component_tags %}
{% component "card" title="My Card" %}
  {% fill "content" %}
    <p>Custom content here.</p>
  {% endfill %}
{% endcomponent %}

From Python:

html = Card.render(kwargs={"title": "My Card"})

docs.title: Component discovery
docs.text:
Components are automatically discovered from:

  • components/ directories inside each installed app
  • Directories listed in COMPONENTS.dirs
  • Modules listed in COMPONENTS.libraries

docs.title: Settings
docs.text:
Configure django-components-lite via the COMPONENTS setting in your Django settings:

from django_components_lite import ComponentsSettings

COMPONENTS = ComponentsSettings(
    autodiscover=True,
    dirs=[BASE_DIR / "components"],
    app_dirs=["components"],
    multiline_tags=True,
    static_files_allowed=[".css", ".js"],
    static_files_forbidden=[".html", ".py"],
)

docs.title: Available settings
docs.text: Setting Default Description autodiscover True Automatically discover components in app directories dirs [BASE_DIR / "components"] Root-level directories to search for components app_dirs ["components"] Subdirectory name within apps to search for components multiline_tags True Allow component tag arguments to span multiple lines static_files_allowed CSS, JS, images, fonts File extensions served as static files static_files_forbidden .html, .py, etc. File extensions never served as static files

docs.title: Slots
docs.text: Slots allow parent templates to inject content into specific areas of a component.

docs.title: Defining slots
docs.text:
Use {% slot %} in your component template:

{% load component_tags %}
<div class="panel">
  <div class="panel-header">
    {% slot "header" %}Default header{% endslot %}
  </div>
  <div class="panel-body">
    {% slot "body" required %}{% endslot %}
  </div>
  <div class="panel-footer">
    {% slot "footer" %}{% endslot %}
  </div>
</div>

docs.title: Filling slots
docs.text:
Use {% fill %} when using the component:

{% load component_tags %}
{% component "panel" %}
  {% fill "header" %}
    <h2>Custom Header</h2>
  {% endfill %}
  {% fill "body" %}
    <p>Panel content goes here.</p>
  {% endfill %}
{% endcomponent %}

docs.title: Default slot
docs.text:
Content placed directly inside {% component %} without a {% fill %} goes into the default slot:

{% component "panel" %}
  This goes into the default slot.
{% endcomponent %}

docs.title: Slot fallback
docs.text:
Content inside {% slot %} is the fallback, rendered when no {% fill %} is provided:

{% slot "sidebar" %}
  <p>Default sidebar content.</p>
{% endslot %}

docs.title: Checking if a slot is filled
docs.text:
In your component's template, you can check whether a slot was filled:

{% load component_tags %}
{% if component_vars.slots.header %}
  <div class="has-header">
    {% slot "header" %}{% endslot %}
  </div>
{% endif %}
